stories.article: Iran launched retaliatory missile strikes on U.S. military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan, escalating tensions between the two countries. The attacks followed a period of increased hostilities, including recent U.S. actions in the region. Both Iran and the United States have intensified their military activities amid ongoing disputes. Additionally, the release of an American detainee has been reported as part of the developments.

headlines.title: Iran launches retaliatory attacks on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan
headlines.lede: The latest missile strikes began when the US said it fired a missile into a Curaçao-flagged oil tanker's funnel after claiming the ship "ignored multiple warnings".

headlines.title: Iran and US step up attacks, release of American in dispute - Reuters
headlines.lede: Iran and US step up attacks, release of American in dispute  ReutersIran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes, Escalating Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz  The New York TimesIran hit at least 3 bases hosting US assets in latest strikes: Visual analysis  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and VideosIran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes  YahooUS mounts sixth straight night of attacks as Iran warns of wider war  Al Jazeera

stories.article: Iran has warned that the Strait of Hormuz is a "red line" following recent US strikes in the region. The Iranian government also vowed to target regional infrastructure if US military actions escalate further. These statements come amid heightened tensions between Iran and the United States.

headlines.title: Iran warns Strait of Hormuz is ‘Red Line’ amid US strikes
headlines.lede: Iran declared on Thursday that the Strait of Hormuz is an inviolable red line, warning it would strike infrastructure across the entire Gulf region if President Trump follows through on threats to...

headlines.lede: Iran vows to 'crush' regional infrastructure if US strikes escalate  Euronews.comLive updates: Iran warns Strait of Hormuz is an ‘unbreakable red line’ as US expands strikes  CNNTracking Trump: Iran threatens regional infrastructure; Trump’s approval rating remains low; NYT challenges subpoenas; and more  The Washington PostIran warns U.S. of Hormuz ‘red line,’ says it will retaliate if Trump carries out threats  CNBCEXCLUSIVE: Iran tells Houthis to close Red Sea gateway if US hits power network, sources say  Reuters

stories.article: Wildfire smoke is expected to worsen in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic regions through Friday, according to weather reports. In Texas Hill Country, flooding has resulted in at least one death as emergency efforts continue. In political developments, former President Trump fired a newly appointed U.S. attorney in Seattle shortly after federal judges made the appointment. Additionally, hundreds of protesters gathered in Kyiv in response to President Zelenskyy’s dismissal of the defense minister.

stories.article: The Land Transport Authority announced plans to replace tactile tiles at pedestrian crossings over the next four years to enhance safety and accessibility. Meanwhile, the police have issued a warning about scam messages related to GST Vouchers that aim to hijack users' Telegram accounts. Authorities advise the public to remain vigilant and avoid clicking on suspicious links.

headlines.title: Tactile tiles at pedestrian crossings to be replaced over next 4 years
headlines.lede: LTA said the tiles at the crossing where a woman slipped and fell were checked and found unimpaired. Read more at straitstimes.com.

headlines.title: Police warn of GST Voucher scam messages to hijack Telegram accounts
headlines.lede: The Ministry of Finance does not have a broadcast channel on Telegram, the police reminded. Read more at straitstimes.com.
